Introduction — You Didn't Fail. Your Schedule Did.
Chapter 1 — The Prep-Once System
Chapter 2 — Breakfasts *(14 recipes)*
Chapter 3 — Lunches *(20 recipes)*
Chapter 4 — Dinners *(26 recipes)*
Chapter 5 — Snacks & Mini Meals *(14 recipes)*
Chapter 6 — Soups & Stews *(13 recipes)*
Chapter 7 — High-Protein Treats *(8 recipes)*
Chapter 8 — Sauces, Marinades & Dressings *(6 recipes)*
Chapter 9 — The 30-Day Meal Plan
Chapter 10 — Your Bonus Toolkit
Appendix A — Full Nutritional Index
Appendix B — Protein Content of 50 Common Foods & Measurement Conversion Charts
Conclusion — You Have the System Now

You have been here before. Monday morning hits and you are motivated. You buy the groceries, you start the plan, and for a few days everything works. Then Wednesday comes with a late meeting, and Thursday the kids are sick, and by Friday you are eating whatever is fastest because you are exhausted and there is nothing ready.
So you tell yourself you failed again. You didn't.
Your schedule failed you. No plan survives contact with a real week unless it was built for one.

Here is the truth about weight loss that nobody puts on the cover of a diet book: the hardest part is not the eating. It is the deciding. Every single day you have to decide what to eat, whether you have the ingredients, whether you have the time to cook it, and whether it fits your goals. That is four decisions per meal, three meals a day, seven days a week. Decision fatigue is real, and it is the reason most diets fall apart before they get a chance to work.
This book eliminates the decisions.

Not by restricting you to one meal or one flavor or one food group. By giving you a system that does the thinking on Sunday so you coast through the week on autopilot. One prep session. One week of meals. Zero nightly stress.
**What makes this book different from every other meal prep book on this shelf:**

Every other book in this category cycles through the same two or three proteins — chicken breast, ground turkey, and maybe some beef on a good week. By Thursday you are eating your fourth chicken bowl and wondering why you even bothered. This book uses six protein sources: chicken, beef, fish, eggs, legumes, and dairy. Real variety. Real flavors. One hundred and one recipes that do not blur together by midweek.

Every recipe in this book delivers 25 grams of protein or more per serving. Not approximately. Not close. At least 25 grams, every single meal. That is the number that keeps you full, preserves your lean muscle while you lose fat, and shuts down the cravings that derail you every afternoon.
And every meal is designed to be prepped in advance, stored safely, and reheated without losing its texture or flavor. Because food that tastes like sad leftovers is food you stop making.
**The 3 rules of this book:**
1. Prep once. Everything you need for the week comes together in one Sunday session of two hours or less.
2. Eat real food. Every ingredient in this book is available at your regular supermarket. No specialty stores, no protein powders required, no $15 bags of something you cannot pronounce.
3. Never be hungry. Twenty-five grams of protein per meal is not negotiable. You will eat enough. You will feel full. And the weight will follow.
**How to use this book:**

Start with Chapter 1. Read it once. It contains everything you need to understand before you cook a single thing — how much protein your body actually needs, which six sources we use and why, and the exact Sunday method that makes the whole system work.

Then go straight to Chapter 9, the 30-Day Meal Plan. It tells you exactly what to cook each week, in what order, and what to buy. You do not need to read every recipe before you start. Just follow the plan.
If you want to customize, use the Mix-and-Match Matrix in Week 4 to build your own combinations once the habits are locked in.

The quiz in the Bonus Toolkit will help you figure out exactly which week to start based on your current lifestyle. If you have never meal prepped before, it will tell you. If you are already comfortable in the kitchen and want to jump to week three, it will tell you that too.
You have the system now. Let's use it.

The number you hear most often is 0.7 to 1 gram of protein per pound of body weight per day. For a 160-pound person, that means roughly 112 to 160 grams of protein daily. For weight loss specifically, staying toward the higher end of that range — around 1 gram per pound — helps preserve the muscle you have while your body burns fat. More muscle means a faster metabolism, which means the weight you lose stays off.
A simpler way to think about it: aim for 25 to 40 grams of protein per meal across three meals a day, plus one or two protein-rich snacks. Every recipe in this book is built to hit that target.
You do not need to count every gram. Follow the plan, use the recipes, and the math takes care of itself.
**Chicken** — lean, versatile, and affordable. Chicken breast and thighs anchor dozens of recipes in this book. Thighs add more flavor and hold up better in meal prep without drying out.
**Beef** — ground beef, sirloin, and stew meat bring iron, zinc, and serious staying power. Used in bowls, stews, and chili throughout.

**Fish** — salmon, cod, tuna, and shrimp deliver omega-3 fatty acids alongside high-quality protein. Salmon in particular is one of the best weight-loss proteins because of its effect on hunger hormones.
**Eggs** — the most complete protein source in any kitchen. Whole eggs, egg whites, and hard-boiled eggs appear throughout the breakfast and snack chapters.

**Legumes** — black beans, white beans, chickpeas, and lentils bring plant-based protein plus fiber, which extends satiety even further. Often paired with animal proteins to boost the total protein count while adding texture.

**Dairy** — Greek yogurt, cottage cheese, and reduced-fat cheese are protein powerhouses hiding in plain sight. Greek yogurt and cottage cheese in particular are used extensively in this book for both savory and sweet recipes.
No single source exceeds 30 percent of the total recipes in this book. That is the variety guarantee.

Every meal in this book follows the same invisible structure: **½ protein + ¼ smart carb + ¼ vegetables**, with a sauce or seasoning that ties it together. You do not need to measure your plate. The recipes are already built this way. Once you see it in the recipes, you will start to see it everywhere.
The entire week of food comes together in one prep session. Here is the sequence that makes it efficient:

**Step 1 — Proteins first (45–60 minutes).** Start with whatever takes the longest. Sheet pan proteins go in the oven. Ground meat goes on the stove. Eggs go in a pot of boiling water. Salmon goes in the oven after the chicken comes out. Everything that requires heat gets started before you touch anything else.
**Step 2 — Grains and legumes while proteins cook (15–20 minutes).** Rice cooker on. Quinoa on the stove. Canned beans drained and rinsed. These are hands-off once they are started.
**Step 3 — Vegetables and assembly (20–25 minutes).** Roast or chop vegetables. Mix sauces. Portion everything into containers.
Total active time: under 2 hours. Total hands-on effort: less than 90 minutes.
**Fridge (up to 4 days):** cooked chicken, ground beef, fish, egg-based dishes, grain bowls, soups, stews, cooked vegetables.
**Freezer (up to 3 months):** chili, soups, stews, meatballs, cooked ground beef, most casseroles. Do not freeze: egg dishes, cooked fish (texture suffers), salads, anything with avocado.
Label every container with the contents and the date. Use glass containers when possible — they reheat evenly and do not absorb odors.
Keep these in your kitchen at all times and you can build a high-protein meal from scratch on any day.

**Proteins:** canned tuna, canned salmon, canned chickpeas, canned black beans, canned white beans, canned lentils, frozen shrimp, frozen chicken breast, eggs, Greek yogurt (plain, full-fat or 2%), cottage cheese, string cheese, deli turkey.
**Grains and bases:** brown rice, quinoa, whole wheat pasta, oats (rolled and steel-cut), whole wheat tortillas, whole grain bread.
**Vegetables:** frozen broccoli, frozen spinach, frozen edamame, canned diced tomatoes, canned corn, garlic, onions, bell peppers, sweet potatoes.
**Fats and flavor:** olive oil, avocado oil, natural peanut butter, almond butter, reduced-sodium soy sauce, hot sauce, Dijon mustard, apple cider vinegar, honey, salsa.
**Seasonings:** garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, cumin, chili powder, Italian seasoning, black pepper, red pepper flakes, dried oregano, dried thyme.

*The biggest mistake people make with breakfast is leaving it to chance. When you open the fridge in the morning and find nothing ready, you grab whatever is fastest — and fast is rarely high-protein. These 14 recipes fix that. Every one is made ahead on Sunday, portioned into containers, and ready to grab on the way out the door or heat up in under three minutes. Protein sources: eggs and dairy.*

content.title: High-Protein Egg & Veggie Muffins
ingredients: ½ cup red bell pepper, diced
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease a 12-cup muffin tin well with cooking spray.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs and cottage cheese until smooth.
Stir in the spinach, bell pepper, mushrooms, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Pour the mixture evenly into the prepared muffin cups, filling each about ¾ full.
Top each cup with a small pinch of shredded cheddar.
Bake for 20–22 minutes until the egg muffins are set in the center and lightly golden on top.
Let cool for 5 minutes before removing. Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 4 days.
content.notes: Make-Ahead Tip: Reheat 3 muffins in the microwave for 60–75 seconds straight from the fridge.

content.title: Greek Yogurt Power Bowl with Berries & Almonds
ingredients: 1½ cups plain Greek yogurt (2% or full-fat)
½ cup mixed berries (fresh or thawed from frozen)
instructions: Spoon the Greek yogurt into a bowl or meal prep container.
Stir in the vanilla extract and chia seeds.
Top with the mixed berries and sliced almonds.
Drizzle honey over the top.
If prepping ahead, keep the berries and almonds in a separate small container and add just before eating to maintain texture.
Store in the fridge for up to 3 days.
content.notes: Swap It: No berries? Use diced peaches, sliced banana, or diced apple.

content.title: Overnight Oats with Cottage Cheese & Chia
ingredients: ½ cup fresh or frozen blueberries
instructions: In a mason jar or airtight container, combine the rolled oats, cottage cheese, almond milk, chia seeds, maple syrup, and cinnamon.
Stir everything together until well combined.
Seal the container and refrigerate overnight or for at least 6 hours.
In the morning, stir well and top with blueberries.
Eat cold or microwave for 90 seconds if you prefer it warm.
Stores in the fridge for up to 4 days — prep the full week on Sunday.
content.notes: Make-Ahead Tip: Prep five jars on Sunday. Breakfast is done for the entire week.

content.title: Turkey Sausage & Sweet Potato Hash
ingredients: 2 medium sweet potatoes, peeled and diced small
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced sweet potatoes and cook for 10–12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tender and lightly browned. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, heat the remaining oil and cook the turkey, breaking it up as it browns, about 6–7 minutes.
Add the bell pepper and onion to the turkey and cook for another 3–4 minutes until softened.
Return the sweet potatoes to the pan, add smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Stir everything together.
Cook for 2 more minutes until everything is heated through and well combined.
Divide into 4 containers. Store in the fridge for up to 4 days.
content.notes: Swap It: Add 2 eggs scrambled in at the end for an extra protein boost.

content.title: Spinach & Feta Egg Bake
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease a 9×13-inch baking dish with cooking spray.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs and cottage cheese until smooth.
Stir in the spinach, feta, garlic powder, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Pour the mixture into the prepared baking dish.
Scatter the cherry tomatoes over the top.
Bake for 28–32 minutes until the center is fully set and the edges are lightly golden.
Let cool for 10 minutes before slicing into 6 portions. Store in airtight containers for up to 4 days.
content.notes: Make-Ahead Tip: Reheat individual portions in the microwave for 90 seconds. Excellent cold too.

content.title: Smoked Salmon & Cottage Cheese Toast
instructions: Toast the bread slices until golden and crisp.
Spread a generous layer of cottage cheese on each slice.
Lay the smoked salmon evenly over the cottage cheese.
Top with cucumber slices and capers if using.
Sprinkle everything bagel seasoning and fresh dill over the top.
Serve immediately. For meal prep, store the components separately and assemble in the morning.
content.notes: Swap It: Use whole wheat crackers instead of toast for a lower-carb version.

content.title: Black Bean Breakfast Scramble
ingredients: 1 cup canned black beans, drained and rinsed
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the black beans and corn, season with cumin and chili powder, and cook for 2–3 minutes until heated through.
Push the beans and corn to the edges of the pan. Pour the eggs into the center.
Scramble the eggs gently, incorporating the beans and corn as they cook.
Remove from heat when the eggs are just set. Stir in the salsa.
Top with shredded cheese and serve. For meal prep, divide into 2 containers and refrigerate for up to 3 days.
content.notes: Make-Ahead Tip: Reheat in a skillet over low heat rather than the microwave to keep the eggs fluffy.

content.title: 3-Ingredient Protein Pancakes
instructions: Combine the cottage cheese, eggs, oats, and cinnamon in a blender. Blend until completely smooth, about 30 seconds.
Let the batter rest for 2 minutes while you heat a nonstick skillet over medium heat. Coat with cooking spray.
Pour about 3 tablespoons of batter per pancake onto the skillet.
Cook for 2–3 minutes until the edges look set and small bubbles form on the surface.
Flip carefully and cook for another 1–2 minutes until golden.
Serve immediately or cool completely before storing. Stack in airtight containers separated by parchment paper. Refrigerate for up to 4 days or freeze for up to 1 month.
content.notes: Make-Ahead Tip: Reheat from frozen in the toaster for 2 minutes — better texture than the microwave.

content.title: Cottage Cheese & Pineapple Bowl
ingredients: ½ cup fresh or canned pineapple chunks (in juice, drained)
instructions: Spoon the cottage cheese into a bowl or meal prep container.
Top with pineapple chunks and walnuts.
Drizzle with honey and sprinkle with ground ginger.
If prepping ahead, keep the pineapple and walnuts separate and add just before eating.
Store in the fridge for up to 4 days.
content.notes: Swap It: Sub mango or peach chunks for the pineapple. Use pecans instead of walnuts.

content.title: Sausage & Egg Breakfast Casserole
ingredients: 1 lb turkey breakfast sausage, casings removed
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease a 9×13-inch baking dish.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook the turkey sausage, breaking it apart, until fully browned. Drain any excess fat.
Spread the hash browns in an even layer in the baking dish. Top with the cooked sausage.
In a bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, garlic powder, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Pour the egg mixture evenly over the sausage and hash browns.
Top with shredded cheddar cheese.
Bake for 35–40 minutes until the center is fully set and the cheese is golden. Cool before slicing into 6 portions.
content.notes: Make-Ahead Tip: Slice and freeze individual portions for up to 1 month. Reheat in the microwave for 2 minutes.

content.title: Peanut Butter Protein Overnight Oats
instructions: In a mason jar or container, combine the oats, Greek yogurt, almond milk, peanut butter, honey, and chia seeds.
Stir until the peanut butter is fully incorporated.
Seal and refrigerate overnight or for at least 6 hours.
In the morning, stir well and top with banana slices.
Eat cold or heat in the microwave for 90 seconds if preferred.
Store in the fridge (without banana) for up to 4 days.
content.notes: Swap It: Sub almond butter for peanut butter. Use diced apple instead of banana.

content.title: Veggie & Cheese Frittata
ingredients: 2 tbsp fresh basil, chopped (or 1 tsp dried)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven broiler to high.
Whisk eggs and milk in a bowl. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a 10-inch oven-safe skillet over medium heat.
Add zucchini and cook for 3–4 minutes until slightly softened.
Add cherry tomatoes and cook for 1 more minute.
Pour the egg mixture over the vegetables. Scatter mozzarella and basil on top.
Cook on the stovetop for 4–5 minutes until the edges are set.
Transfer to the broiler for 3–4 minutes until the top is puffed and golden. Slice into 4 wedges.
content.notes: Note: Pairs well with a side of Greek yogurt or cottage cheese to hit the 25g protein target.

content.title: High-Protein Banana Smoothie Bowl
ingredients: Toppings: sliced almonds, granola, fresh berries
instructions: Combine the frozen banana, Greek yogurt, cottage cheese, almond butter, and almond milk in a blender.
Blend until thick and smooth. Add more almond milk one tablespoon at a time if needed for blending.
The consistency should be much thicker than a drinkable smoothie — you want to eat it with a spoon.
Pour into a bowl and add desired toppings.
For meal prep, blend without toppings and store in a container for up to 2 days. Add toppings just before eating.
content.notes: Make-Ahead Tip: Pre-portion the frozen banana, yogurt, and cottage cheese into freezer bags. Blend each morning in under 2 minutes.

content.title: Cottage Cheese Scrambled Eggs
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Crack the eggs into a bowl, add the cottage cheese, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Whisk together.
Melt the butter in a nonstick skillet over low to medium-low heat.
Pour in the egg mixture. Let it sit for 30 seconds without touching it.
Using a rubber spatula, slowly push the eggs from the edges toward the center in large folds.
Continue folding gently every 20–30 seconds. Remove from heat just before the eggs look fully set — residual heat will finish cooking them.
Top with chives and serve. Store in the fridge for up to 2 days.
content.notes: Lunch is where most people fall apart. When noon hits and there is nothing ready, takeout wins by default. These 20 make-ahead lunches stay fresh in the fridge for four days, travel well in any container, and keep hunger locked down until dinner. Every single one delivers 25 grams of protein or more. Dominant protein sources: chicken and fish.

content.title: Greek Chicken Meal Prep Bowls
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Slice chicken breast into strips. Toss with olive oil, oregano, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Heat a skillet or grill pan over medium-high heat. Cook chicken strips for 4–5 minutes per side until fully cooked and slightly charred. Rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Divide the brown rice equally among 4 meal prep containers.
Top each with chicken, cherry tomatoes, cucumber, and olives.
Sprinkle feta cheese over each bowl.
Store in the fridge for up to 4 days. Add dressing just before eating.
content.notes: Make-Ahead Tip: Pair with the Greek Yogurt Tzatziki from Chapter 8 for a complete meal.

content.title: Chipotle Turkey & Black Bean Wraps
ingredients: 1 can (15 oz) black beans, drained and rinsed
1 tbsp chipotle seasoning (or 1 tsp cumin + 1 tsp chili powder + ½ tsp smoked paprika)
½ cup shredded Mexican blend cheese
instructions: In a skillet over medium-high heat, cook the ground turkey, breaking it apart, until fully browned, about 8 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Add the black beans and chipotle seasoning. Stir to combine and cook for 2 minutes.
Stir in the salsa and cook for another 2 minutes until the mixture is slightly thickened.
Warm the tortillas briefly in the microwave for 20 seconds.
Divide the turkey and bean mixture among the 4 tortillas. Top with lettuce and cheese.
Wrap tightly. For meal prep, wrap in foil or store filling and tortillas separately.
content.notes: Make-Ahead Tip: Store the filling in the fridge and assemble wraps each morning to keep tortillas from getting soggy.

content.title: Tuna & White Bean Salad Jars
ingredients: 3 cans (5 oz each) tuna in water, drained
1 can (15 oz) white beans, drained and rinsed
¼ cup red onion, finely diced
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the tuna, white beans, celery, and red onion.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, red wine vinegar, and Dijon mustard until emulsified.
Pour the dressing over the tuna and bean mixture. Toss gently to coat.
Season with salt and pepper.
Divide among 4 mason jars or airtight containers. Refrigerate for up to 4 days.
Serve over mixed greens, in a wrap, or straight from the jar.
content.notes: Swap It: Use canned salmon instead of tuna for a higher omega-3 version.

content.title: Lemon Herb Salmon Salad
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with foil.
Place salmon on the baking sheet. Drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil, season with salt, pepper, dill, and garlic powder.
Bake for 12–15 minutes until the salmon flakes easily with a fork. Let cool completely before flaking.
Whisk together the remaining 3 tbsp olive oil and lemon juice to make the dressing.
For meal prep, divide the greens, tomatoes, and cucumber among 4 containers. Store salmon and dressing separately.
Assemble just before eating to keep the greens crisp.

content.title: Chicken Caesar Meal Prep Jars
ingredients: 1½ lbs chicken breast, grilled and sliced
½ cup High-Protein Caesar Dressing (Chapter 8, Recipe 98)
instructions: Grill or pan-sear seasoned chicken breast until fully cooked. Cool completely before slicing.
Layer each mason jar in this order from bottom: 2 tbsp dressing, sliced chicken, Parmesan, croutons, then romaine on top.
Seal and refrigerate for up to 4 days. When ready to eat, shake the jar to distribute the dressing or tip into a bowl.
The dressing stays at the bottom and the lettuce at the top keeps everything crisp.

content.title: Shrimp & Mango Rice Bowls
ingredients: ½ cup red bell pepper, diced
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Toss shrimp with olive oil, chili powder, salt, and pepper.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook shrimp for 1–2 minutes per side until pink and just cooked through. Do not overcook. Let cool.
In a small bowl, mix lime juice with a pinch of salt to make a quick dressing.
Divide brown rice among 4 containers. Top with shrimp, mango, cucumber, and bell pepper.
Drizzle lime dressing over each bowl. Store dressing separately if prepping ahead.

content.title: Egg Salad Lettuce Wraps
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Hard-boil eggs: place in cold water, bring to a boil, cook for 10 minutes, then transfer to an ice bath. Peel and chop.
In a bowl, combine the chopped eggs, Greek yogurt, Dijon mustard, celery, and red onion.
Season with smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Mix gently.
Store the egg salad in an airtight container for up to 3 days.
To serve, spoon egg salad into butter lettuce cups. Two lettuce wraps per serving.
content.notes: Note: Serve alongside a side of cottage cheese to boost protein to 30g+ per meal.

content.title: Chickpea & Roasted Veggie Power Bowl
ingredients: 2 cans (15 oz each) chickpeas, drained and rinsed
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Line a large baking sheet with foil.
Toss chickpeas and all vegetables with olive oil, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper.
Spread in a single layer on the baking sheet. Roast for 22–25 minutes until chickpeas are crispy and vegetables are golden.
Divide quinoa among 4 containers. Top with roasted chickpeas and veggies.
Sprinkle feta over each bowl. Drizzle with olive oil or the Avocado Lime Crema from Chapter 8.
content.notes: Note: Add 1 cup Greek yogurt on the side to bring total protein to 43g.

content.title: Buffalo Chicken Lettuce Cups
instructions: Poach chicken breast in simmering water for 18–20 minutes until cooked through. Shred with two forks.
In a bowl, mix hot sauce and melted butter. Toss shredded chicken in the buffalo sauce.
In a separate small bowl, mix Greek yogurt with a pinch of salt to make a quick cooling sauce.
To serve, spoon buffalo chicken into romaine leaves. Top with celery, carrots, and a drizzle of the yogurt sauce.
Store all components separately in the fridge for up to 4 days. Assemble just before eating.

content.title: Tuna Pasta Salad
ingredients: 3 cans (5 oz each) tuna in water, drained
2 cups whole wheat rotini, cooked and cooled
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Cook pasta according to package directions. Drain, rinse with cold water, and let cool completely.
In a large bowl, combine the cooled pasta, tuna, peas, red onion, and pickle.
In a small bowl, mix together Greek yogurt, mayonnaise, and Dijon mustard.
Pour the dressing over the pasta mixture and toss to coat evenly.
Season with salt and pepper. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving.
Store in an airtight container for up to 4 days.

content.title: Turkey & Quinoa Stuffed Peppers (Cold)
ingredients: 4 large bell peppers, halved and seeded
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F.
Cook the ground turkey in a skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it apart, until fully browned. Drain fat.
Stir in the diced tomatoes, cooked quinoa, Italian seasoning, and garlic powder. Season with salt and pepper.
Arrange the pepper halves in a baking dish. Fill each with the turkey-quinoa mixture.
Top each pepper with shredded mozzarella.
Bake for 25–30 minutes until the peppers are tender and the cheese is golden. Store in the fridge for up to 4 days.

content.title: Asian Chicken Noodle Salad
ingredients: 1½ lbs chicken breast, grilled and shredded
1 tsp fresh ginger, grated (or ½ tsp ground ginger)
instructions: Cook pasta according to package directions. Drain, rinse with cold water, and cool completely.
In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, sesame oil, rice vinegar, and ginger.
Add the shredded chicken, cooled noodles, cabbage, carrots, and edamame to the bowl.
Toss everything together until well coated.
Divide among 4 containers. Refrigerate for up to 4 days. This salad actually tastes better the next day as the flavors develop.

content.title: White Bean & Tuna Salad
ingredients: 2 cans (5 oz each) tuna in water, drained
2 cans (15 oz each) white cannellini beans, drained and rinsed
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: In a large bowl, gently combine the tuna and white beans, being careful not to mash the beans.
Add the olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, parsley, and red pepper flakes.
Toss gently until everything is coated. Season with salt and pepper.
Let the salad rest for at least 15 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld.
Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 4 days.

content.title: Beef & Brown Rice Power Bowls
instructions: In a large skillet over medium-high heat, cook the ground beef, breaking it apart, until fully browned. Drain excess fat.
Add garlic powder, ground ginger, soy sauce, and sesame oil to the beef. Stir to coat and cook for 2 more minutes.
Divide brown rice among 4 meal prep containers.
Top each bowl with the seasoned beef, steamed broccoli, and shredded carrots.
Garnish with sliced green onions. Store in the fridge for up to 4 days.

content.title: Chicken & Avocado Protein Wraps
instructions: Mash the avocados with lime juice, salt, and pepper.
Mix the Greek yogurt into the mashed avocado until smooth.
Stir in the cilantro and corn.
Warm the tortillas in the microwave for 20 seconds.
Spread the avocado-yogurt mixture on each tortilla.
Top with shredded chicken and romaine lettuce.
Roll up tightly. For meal prep, store the avocado mixture and chicken separately and assemble daily to prevent browning.

content.title: Lentil & Veggie Mason Jar Salad
ingredients: ½ cup red bell pepper, diced
¼ cup red onion, finely diced
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Rinse lentils and cook in 3 cups water for 18–20 minutes until tender but not mushy. Drain and cool completely.
Whisk together olive oil, red wine vinegar, oregano, salt, and pepper.
In each mason jar, layer from bottom: 2 tbsp dressing, lentils, bell pepper, red onion, cherry tomatoes, cucumber, then feta on top.
Seal and refrigerate for up to 5 days. Shake before eating or tip into a bowl and toss.
content.notes: Note: Add a 4-oz side of grilled chicken to hit 40g+ protein.

content.title: Teriyaki Chicken Rice Bowls
ingredients: 1½ lbs chicken thighs, boneless and skinless
instructions: Whisk soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, garlic powder, and cornstarch in a small bowl.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat with a splash of oil. Cook chicken thighs for 5–6 minutes per side until fully cooked. Let rest for 5 minutes, then slice.
Pour the sauce into the pan over medium heat. Cook for 2–3 minutes until it thickens slightly.
Toss the sliced chicken in the teriyaki sauce.
Divide brown rice among 4 containers. Top with chicken and broccoli. Drizzle remaining sauce over each bowl. Sprinkle sesame seeds.

content.title: Shrimp & Avocado Salad
ingredients: ½ cup corn, fresh or thawed
¼ cup red onion, finely diced
instructions: Toss shrimp with olive oil, chili powder, salt, and pepper. Cook in a skillet over medium-high heat for 1–2 minutes per side until pink. Cool completely.
In a large bowl, combine cooled shrimp, avocado, cherry tomatoes, corn, and red onion.
Drizzle with lime juice and toss gently.
For meal prep, store avocado separately and add just before eating to prevent browning.
Refrigerate for up to 3 days.

content.title: Greek Turkey Meatball Bowls
ingredients: 2 cups cooked farro or brown rice
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Combine ground turkey, breadcrumbs, egg, garlic powder, oregano, salt, and pepper. Mix just until combined.
Roll into 20 equal meatballs. Place on a lined baking sheet.
Bake for 18–20 minutes until cooked through and lightly browned.
Divide farro among 4 containers. Top with 5 meatballs each, cucumber, tomatoes, and feta.
Serve with tzatziki from Chapter 8.

content.title: Salmon & Cucumber Rice Paper Rolls
ingredients: 2 cans (5 oz each) salmon in water, drained
instructions: Mix soy sauce, sesame oil, and rice vinegar in a small bowl for the dipping sauce.
Fill a wide, shallow bowl with warm water. Dip one rice paper sheet for 10–15 seconds until pliable.
Lay flat. Place a line of salmon, avocado, cucumber, and carrots across the lower third.
Fold the bottom up over the filling, tuck in the sides, and roll tightly.
Repeat with remaining wrappers. Serve with dipping sauce.
For meal prep, wrap individually in damp paper towels and store in a sealed container for up to 2 days.
content.notes: Dinner is the most dangerous meal for anyone trying to lose weight. You are tired, hungry, and decisions feel impossible. These 26 dinners remove the decision entirely — everything is already cooked, portioned, and waiting. From one-pan salmon to slow cooker chili to creamy Tuscan chicken, every recipe was designed to taste like real cooking, not diet food. Dominant protein sources: beef, chicken, and fish.

content.title: Garlic Butter Salmon Tray Bake
ingredients: 1½ lbs salmon fillet, cut into 4 portions
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a large baking sheet with foil.
Mix melted butter with minced garlic, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Arrange asparagus on one side of the baking sheet. Lay salmon portions on the other side.
Brush the garlic butter generously over both the salmon and asparagus.
Place lemon slices on top of the salmon.
Bake for 15–18 minutes until the salmon flakes easily and asparagus is tender. Serve immediately or cool and store in the fridge for up to 3 days.
content.notes: Make-Ahead Tip: Pairs perfectly with brown rice prepped on Sunday. Store separately and reheat the salmon gently.

content.title: Beef Power Bowls with Roasted Peppers
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Toss sliced peppers and onion with 1 tbsp olive oil, smoked paprika, and salt. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 20–25 minutes until caramelized.
In a skillet over medium-high heat, cook the ground beef in remaining oil with garlic powder, cumin, salt, and pepper until fully browned.
Divide brown rice among 4 containers. Top with beef and roasted peppers.
Store in the fridge for up to 4 days.

content.title: Mediterranean Turkey Meatballs with Tzatziki
ingredients: 1 cup cooked quinoa (per serving, as base)
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with foil.
In a bowl, combine ground turkey, breadcrumbs, egg, garlic, oregano, mint, feta, salt, and pepper. Mix gently — do not overwork the meat.
Shape into 24 equal meatballs. Arrange on the baking sheet without touching.
Bake for 18–20 minutes until golden and cooked through (internal temp 165°F).
Serve over quinoa with Tzatziki from Chapter 8. Store meatballs in the fridge for up to 4 days or freeze for up to 2 months.

content.title: Slow Cooker Chicken & White Bean Stew
ingredients: 2 lbs chicken thighs, boneless and skinless
2 cans (15 oz each) white beans, drained
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Add chicken thighs, white beans, diced tomatoes, chicken broth, garlic, thyme, and rosemary to a slow cooker.
Season with salt and pepper. Stir briefly.
Cover and cook on LOW for 6–8 hours or HIGH for 3–4 hours.
Use two forks to shred the chicken directly in the slow cooker.
Stir in the fresh spinach during the last 10 minutes before serving.
Portion into containers. Refrigerate for up to 4 days or freeze for up to 3 months.

content.title: Sheet Pan Chicken Thighs & Asparagus
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Pat chicken thighs dry with paper towels. Mix olive oil with garlic powder, paprika, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper.
Coat chicken thighs with half the seasoned oil. Place skin-side up on a large baking sheet.
Roast for 20 minutes. Remove from oven and add asparagus alongside the chicken, tossing in remaining oil.
Return to oven for another 10–12 minutes until chicken skin is crispy and internal temperature reaches 165°F.
Store in the fridge for up to 4 days.

content.title: Ground Turkey Stuffed Bell Peppers
ingredients: 4 large bell peppers, tops cut off and seeded
1 can (15 oz) diced tomatoes with green chilies
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F.
Brown ground turkey in a skillet over medium-high heat. Drain fat.
Stir in diced tomatoes, cooked rice, Italian seasoning, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Stand bell peppers in a baking dish. Fill each with the turkey and rice mixture, packing it in well.
Top each pepper with shredded mozzarella.
Pour ½ cup water into the bottom of the baking dish. Cover tightly with foil.
Bake for 25 minutes covered, then uncover and bake 10 more minutes until cheese is melted and golden.

content.title: Beef & Lentil One-Pot Stew
instructions: Brown ground beef in a large pot over medium-high heat. Drain fat.
Add onion, carrots, celery, and garlic. Cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in lentils, diced tomatoes, beef broth, paprika, and cumin. Season with salt and pepper.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Simmer covered for 30 minutes until lentils are tender.
Adjust seasoning and serve. Store in the fridge for up to 4 days or freeze for up to 3 months.

content.title: Creamy Tuscan Chicken
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Season chicken with Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken for 5–6 minutes per side until golden. Remove and let rest.
In the same skillet, sauté garlic for 1 minute. Add chicken broth and bring to a simmer.
Remove from heat briefly and stir in Greek yogurt and Parmesan until smooth.
Return to low heat. Add sun-dried tomatoes and spinach. Stir until spinach wilts.
Slice the chicken and return to the pan. Simmer for 2–3 minutes. Serve over pasta, rice, or cauliflower rice.

content.title: Black Bean & Beef Chili
ingredients: 2 cans (15 oz each) black beans, drained
instructions: Brown ground beef in a large pot over medium-high heat with the onion and garlic. Drain fat.
Add chili powder, cumin, and smoked paprika. Stir and cook for 1 minute.
Add crushed tomatoes, black beans, and beef broth. Stir to combine.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. Cook uncovered for 25–30 minutes until thickened.
Season with salt and pepper. Portion into 8 containers.
Freeze individual portions for up to 3 months. This is the best freezer meal in the book.

content.title: Teriyaki Salmon with Edamame Rice
ingredients: 1½ lbs salmon fillets, cut into 4 portions
instructions: Whisk soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, and garlic powder for the teriyaki sauce.
Marinate salmon in half the sauce for 10 minutes.
Heat a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Cook salmon skin-side down for 3–4 minutes. Flip and cook 2–3 more minutes. Pour remaining sauce over salmon in the last minute.
Mix cooked rice and edamame together.
Divide rice mixture among 4 containers. Top with salmon and sprinkle sesame seeds.

content.title: Lemon Baked Cod with Roasted Broccoli
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Toss broccoli with 2 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet.
Roast broccoli for 10 minutes.
Push broccoli to the sides and place cod fillets in the center. Mix remaining olive oil with lemon juice, garlic, and dill. Brush over cod.
Return to oven for another 10–12 minutes until the cod flakes easily.
Store broccoli and cod separately in the fridge for up to 3 days.

content.title: Chicken & Broccoli Stir-Fry
instructions: Mix soy sauce, sesame oil, cornstarch, garlic powder, and ginger in a bowl. Add chicken slices and toss to coat.
Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat with a splash of oil.
Cook chicken in a single layer for 2–3 minutes per side until cooked through. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, stir-fry broccoli and carrots for 3–4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Return chicken to the pan. Add any remaining marinade and toss everything together for 1–2 minutes.
Serve over brown rice. Store in the fridge for up to 4 days.

content.title: Pork Tenderloin with Apple & Dijon
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Mix Dijon mustard, honey, thyme, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Rub all over the pork tenderloin.
Heat olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over high heat. Sear the pork for 2 minutes per side until browned on all surfaces.
Arrange apple slices around the pork in the skillet.
Transfer to the oven. Roast for 18–20 minutes until internal temperature reaches 145°F.
Let rest for 5 minutes before slicing. Serve with the pan apples and juices.

content.title: Turkey Taco Bowls
ingredients: ½ cup plain Greek yogurt (as sour cream substitute)
½ cup shredded Mexican cheese blend
instructions: Brown ground turkey in a skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it apart. Drain fat.
Add taco seasoning and ¼ cup water. Stir and cook for 2 minutes until sauce thickens.
Add black beans and cook for another 2 minutes until heated through.
Divide brown rice among 4 containers. Top with turkey-bean mixture.
Store separately: romaine, salsa, Greek yogurt, and cheese. Add toppings just before eating.

content.title: Garlic Shrimp & Zucchini Noodles
ingredients: 4 medium zucchini, spiralized (or use store-bought zucchini noodles)
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and red pepper flakes. Cook for 30 seconds.
Add shrimp. Cook 1–2 minutes per side until pink. Remove shrimp and set aside.
Add zucchini noodles to the same pan. Pour in chicken broth and lemon juice. Toss for 2–3 minutes — they cook fast. Do not overcook.
Return shrimp to the pan. Toss together. Top with Parmesan.
Best eaten fresh. If prepping ahead, store shrimp and zoodles separately and combine when reheating.

content.title: Honey Mustard Chicken Thighs
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Whisk together Dijon mustard, honey, apple cider vinegar, garlic powder, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Pat chicken thighs dry. Coat thoroughly with the honey-mustard mixture.
Arrange skin-side up on a foil-lined baking sheet.
Roast for 28–32 minutes until the skin is golden and caramelized and internal temperature reaches 165°F.
Store in the fridge for up to 4 days. Reheat in the oven at 375°F for 10 minutes to restore crispiness.

content.title: Beef & Broccoli with Brown Rice
ingredients: 1½ lbs sirloin steak, thinly sliced against the grain
instructions: In a bowl, combine soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, cornstarch, and garlic powder.
Toss sliced beef in half the sauce. Let marinate for 5 minutes.
Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat until smoking. Cook beef in batches for 1–2 minutes until seared. Remove.
Stir-fry broccoli for 3–4 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp.
Return beef to the pan. Add remaining sauce. Toss everything together for 1 minute.
Serve over brown rice. Store in the fridge for up to 4 days.

content.title: Baked Turkey Meatloaf
ingredients: ¼ cup low-sodium ketchup (plus 2 tbsp for topping)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a loaf pan with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, combine all ingredients (except the 2 tbsp ketchup for topping). Mix gently until just combined.
Transfer to the loaf pan and shape into a loaf. Spread the remaining ketchup on top.
Bake for 50–55 minutes until the internal temperature reaches 165°F.
Let rest 10 minutes before slicing into 6 portions. Store in the fridge for up to 4 days or freeze slices for up to 2 months.

content.title: Salmon & Sweet Potato Sheet Pan
ingredients: 1½ lbs salmon fillet, cut into 4 portions
2 medium sweet potatoes, peeled and cubed
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Toss sweet potatoes and Brussels sprouts with 2 tbsp olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Spread on a large baking sheet.
Roast for 15 minutes.
Push vegetables to the sides. Add salmon to the center. Brush salmon with remaining olive oil mixed with maple syrup.
Return to oven for 10–12 minutes until salmon flakes and sweet potatoes are tender.

content.title: Chicken Tikka-Style Skillet
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Cook onion for 5 minutes until softened. Add garlic and cook 1 minute.
Add garam masala, cumin, and paprika. Stir and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add chicken cubes. Cook for 5–6 minutes until lightly browned on the outside.
Pour in crushed tomatoes. Stir to combine. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in Greek yogurt. Do not boil after adding yogurt.
Serve over brown rice or with warm whole wheat naan.

content.title: Beef Taco Skillet
ingredients: 1 can (14.5 oz) diced tomatoes with green chilies
instructions: Brown ground beef in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Drain excess fat.
Add chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, and salt. Stir to coat.
Add black beans, corn, and diced tomatoes. Stir and bring to a simmer.
Cook for 5–7 minutes until slightly thickened.
Sprinkle cheddar cheese over the top, cover, and let melt for 2 minutes.
Serve over rice, in tortillas, or with a side salad. Store in the fridge for up to 4 days.

content.title: Lemon Garlic Chicken Breast
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Mix lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, oregano, thyme, salt, and pepper to make the marinade.
Coat chicken breasts in the marinade. Let sit for at least 10 minutes (or overnight for more flavor).
Heat a grill pan or skillet over medium-high heat with a splash of oil.
Cook chicken for 5–7 minutes per side until fully cooked (internal temp 165°F).
Let rest 5 minutes before slicing. This is the perfect "base protein" for bowls, salads, and wraps. Store in the fridge for up to 4 days.

content.title: Shrimp & Cauliflower Rice Stir-Fry
ingredients: 2 cups frozen peas and carrots, thawed
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add shrimp. Cook 1–2 minutes per side until pink. Remove and set aside.
Push everything to the side and scramble the eggs in the center of the pan.
Add cauliflower rice and peas and carrots. Stir-fry for 3–4 minutes.
Return shrimp to the pan. Add soy sauce. Toss everything together for 1 minute.

content.title: Turkey Bolognese with Whole Wheat Pasta
ingredients: 2 tbsp Parmesan cheese (per serving, for topping)
instructions: Cook pasta per package directions. Drain and reserve ½ cup pasta water.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Cook onion for 5 minutes until soft. Add garlic for 1 minute.
Add turkey, breaking it apart. Cook until fully browned, about 8 minutes. Drain fat.
Add Italian seasoning and marinara. Stir and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add pasta to the sauce. Toss to coat, adding pasta water as needed.
Divide into 6 containers and refrigerate for up to 4 days.

content.title: Slow Cooker Beef & Bean Stew
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Place all ingredients in the slow cooker. Stir briefly to combine.
Cook on LOW for 8 hours or HIGH for 4 hours until the beef is fork-tender and falling apart.
Taste and adjust seasoning before serving.
Portion into 6 containers. Refrigerate for up to 4 days or freeze for up to 3 months.

content.title: Greek Chicken Thighs with Olives & Tomatoes
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Season chicken thighs with oregano, rosemary, salt, and pepper.
Heat olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over high heat. Sear chicken skin-side down for 4–5 minutes until golden. Flip.
Add cherry tomatoes, olives, garlic, and lemon slices around the chicken.
Transfer to the oven. Roast for 20–22 minutes until cooked through.
Spoon the pan juices over the chicken before serving. Store in the fridge for up to 4 days.
content.notes: The window between lunch and dinner is where most diets fall apart. Blood sugar drops, willpower drops with it, and the vending machine wins. These 14 high-protein snacks eliminate that window entirely. Keep two or three prepped in the fridge at all times and the afternoon danger zone becomes a non-issue. Dominant protein sources: eggs, dairy, and legumes.

content.title: No-Bake Peanut Butter Protein Balls
instructions: In a large bowl, combine all ingredients. Mix until everything is well incorporated. The mixture should be thick and hold together when pressed.
If the mixture is too sticky, refrigerate for 15 minutes before rolling.
Roll into 20 equal balls, about 1 inch in diameter.
Place on a parchment-lined tray and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes until firm.
Transfer to an airtight container. Store in the fridge for up to 1 week or freeze for up to 1 month.
content.notes: Note: Pair with a cup of Greek yogurt or a hard-boiled egg to bring total protein to 25g+.

content.title: Hard-Boiled Egg & Avocado Snack Box
instructions: Hard-boil eggs: place in cold water, bring to a boil, cover and remove from heat. Let sit 10 minutes. Transfer to ice bath. Peel.
Halve the avocado. Season with everything bagel seasoning, red pepper flakes, and a squeeze of lemon.
Arrange 4 eggs and avocado half in a meal prep container.
Prep up to 6 eggs at once on Sunday. Store peeled eggs in cold water in the fridge for up to 5 days.

content.title: Turkey Roll-Ups with Hummus
ingredients: ½ cup roasted red peppers, sliced
¼ cucumber, cut into thin sticks
instructions: Lay turkey slices flat on a clean surface.
Spread a thin layer of hummus on each slice.
Place a few spinach leaves, a strip of roasted red pepper, and a cucumber stick at one end.
Roll the turkey tightly around the filling.
Secure with a toothpick if needed. Arrange in a meal prep container.
Store in the fridge for up to 3 days.

content.title: Cottage Cheese & Cucumber Dip
ingredients: 1 tbsp fresh dill (or 1 tsp dried dill)
Salt and black pepper to taste
Vegetable dippers: carrots, celery, bell pepper strips
instructions: Blend the cottage cheese briefly in a blender or food processor for a creamier texture (optional — it works chunky too).
Stir in the cucumber, dill, garlic, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Transfer to a container. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow flavors to develop.
Store for up to 4 days. Serve alongside vegetable dippers.

content.title: Greek Yogurt Ranch Dip with Veggies
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
Veggie dippers: 4 cups mixed raw vegetables
instructions: In a bowl, stir together Greek yogurt, dill, garlic powder, onion powder, parsley, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Taste and adjust seasoning.
Divide into 4 small containers with the veggie dippers packaged alongside.
Store in the fridge for up to 5 days.
content.notes: Note: Pair with 2 hard-boiled eggs to bring total protein to 26g.

content.title: Tuna-Stuffed Mini Peppers
ingredients: 12 mini sweet peppers, halved lengthwise and seeded
2 cans (5 oz each) tuna in water, drained
instructions: In a bowl, combine tuna, Greek yogurt, lemon juice, parsley, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Spoon the tuna mixture into each pepper half.
Arrange in a meal prep container in a single layer.
Store in the fridge for up to 3 days.

content.title: High-Protein Edamame with Sea Salt
instructions: Bring a pot of water to a boil. Add edamame and cook for 4–5 minutes.
Drain and transfer to a bowl.
While still warm, toss with sea salt, garlic powder, and smoked paprika.
Divide into 2 containers. Serve warm or cold.
Store in the fridge for up to 4 days.
content.notes: Note: Pair with a cheese stick and ½ cup cottage cheese for a 30g protein snack.

content.title: Almond & String Cheese Snack Pack
ingredients: ½ cup grapes or apple slices
instructions: Arrange all components in a small divided container or snack bag.
No preparation required beyond portioning.
Prep up to 5 packs on Sunday. Store in the fridge for the entire week.
Grab and go in the morning. Zero effort, consistent protein.

content.title: Smoked Salmon Cucumber Rounds
ingredients: 1 large cucumber, sliced into ¼-inch rounds (about 20 slices)
4 oz smoked salmon, torn into pieces
instructions: Spread a small amount of cottage cheese on each cucumber round.
Top with a piece of smoked salmon.
Garnish with a caper, a pinch of everything bagel seasoning, and a sprig of dill.
Arrange on a plate or in a container. Best assembled same-day.
Store components separately for up to 3 days.

content.title: Roasted Chickpeas
ingredients: 2 cans (15 oz each) chickpeas, drained, rinsed, and dried
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with foil.
The key step: dry the chickpeas thoroughly with paper towels. The drier they are, the crunchier they get.
Toss chickpeas with olive oil and all seasonings. Spread in a single layer on the baking sheet.
Roast for 25–30 minutes, shaking the pan halfway through, until golden and crunchy.
Let cool completely before storing. Keep in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 5 days.
content.notes: Note: Combine with Greek yogurt for a crunch-and-cream snack that hits 25g+ protein.

content.title: Cottage Cheese & Tomato Toast
instructions: Toast bread until golden and crisp.
Spread cottage cheese generously on each slice.
Top with halved cherry tomatoes.
Drizzle balsamic glaze and sprinkle with oregano, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper.
Eat immediately. Store components separately for meal prep and assemble in the morning.

content.title: Turkey & Cheese Roll-Ups
instructions: Lay turkey slices flat. Place a slice of cheese on each.
Spread a thin layer of Dijon mustard on the cheese.
Add a few slices of avocado and a small lettuce leaf.
Roll up tightly and secure with a toothpick.
Store in a container in the fridge for up to 3 days.

content.title: Greek Yogurt with Honey & Walnuts
instructions: Spoon Greek yogurt into a bowl.
Top with walnuts and a sprinkle of cinnamon.
Drizzle honey over the top.
Add berries if using.
For meal prep, pre-portion yogurt into containers. Add dry toppings in a small separate bag.
content.notes: Note: Pair with 2 turkey roll-ups for a 30g+ protein snack combination.

content.title: Protein-Packed Trail Mix
ingredients: ½ cup dried cranberries (no sugar added)
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a large bowl and toss to mix evenly.
Portion into 8 small zip-lock bags or small containers (about ⅓ cup per serving).
Store at room temperature for up to 2 weeks or in the fridge for up to 1 month.
content.notes: Soups and stews are the unsung heroes of meal prep. They get better every day in the fridge, they freeze perfectly, and one big batch feeds you for an entire week. Every recipe in this chapter makes 6+ servings, delivers 25g+ protein per bowl, and works as a complete meal straight from the container. Dominant protein sources: beef, chicken, and legumes.

content.title: High-Protein Chicken Noodle Soup
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Add chicken breast, broth, onion, garlic, thyme, and parsley to a large pot. Bring to a boil.
Reduce to a simmer. Cook for 18–20 minutes until chicken is fully cooked. Remove chicken and shred.
Add carrots, celery, and noodles to the broth. Simmer for 8–10 minutes until tender.
Return shredded chicken to the pot. Season with salt and pepper.
Portion into 6 containers. Refrigerate for up to 4 days or freeze for up to 3 months.

content.title: White Bean & Kale Turkey Soup
ingredients: 2 cans (15 oz each) white cannellini beans, drained
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Cook onion for 5 minutes. Add garlic for 1 minute.
Add turkey and cook, breaking it apart, until fully browned. Drain fat.
Add diced tomatoes, white beans, chicken broth, and Italian seasoning. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Stir in kale and cook for 5 more minutes until wilted. Season with salt and pepper.

content.title: Spicy Beef & Lentil Stew
instructions: Brown ground beef in a large pot over medium-high heat with onion and garlic. Drain fat.
Add cumin, smoked paprika, cayenne, and turmeric. Stir and cook for 1 minute.
Add lentils, diced tomatoes, and beef broth. Stir well.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. Cook for 25–30 minutes until lentils are completely tender and stew has thickened.
Season with salt and pepper. Add more cayenne to taste.

content.title: Creamy Broccoli & Cheddar Protein Soup
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Cook onion for 5 minutes. Add garlic for 1 minute.
Add broth and broccoli. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 12–15 minutes until broccoli is very tender.
Use an immersion blender to blend until smooth. Or blend in batches, leaving some chunks if preferred.
Return to low heat. Stir in blended cottage cheese and shredded cheddar until fully melted and smooth.
Season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Do not boil after adding dairy.

content.title: Shrimp & Corn Chowder
ingredients: 2 medium potatoes, peeled and diced small
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Cook onion and garlic for 5 minutes.
Add potatoes and broth. Bring to a boil and cook for 10 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Add corn and smoked paprika. Simmer for 3 minutes.
Remove from heat. Stir in Greek yogurt until incorporated. Do not return to a boil.
Add shrimp to the hot soup. The residual heat will cook the shrimp in 2–3 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper.

content.title: Smoky Chickpea & Chicken Stew
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Brown chicken cubes on all sides, about 6 minutes total. Remove.
Cook onion and garlic in the same pot for 5 minutes.
Add smoked paprika and cumin. Stir for 30 seconds.
Return chicken to the pot. Add chickpeas, crushed tomatoes, and broth. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Stir in spinach and cook for 2 more minutes until wilted.

content.title: Beef & Vegetable Minestrone
ingredients: 1 cup whole wheat elbow pasta, dry
instructions: Brown ground beef with onion in a large pot over medium-high heat. Drain fat.
Add carrots, celery, Italian seasoning, and broth. Bring to a boil.
Add zucchini, kidney beans, diced tomatoes, and pasta. Reduce to a simmer.
Cook for 15–18 minutes until pasta and vegetables are tender.
Season with salt and pepper. Store in the fridge for up to 4 days or freeze.

content.title: Turkey & Sweet Potato Chili
ingredients: 2 medium sweet potatoes, peeled and diced
instructions: Cook turkey and onion in a large pot over medium-high heat until turkey is browned. Drain fat.
Add chili powder, cumin, and smoked paprika. Stir for 1 minute.
Add sweet potatoes, black beans, crushed tomatoes, and broth. Bring to a boil.
Reduce to a simmer. Cook for 20 minutes until sweet potatoes are tender.
Season with salt and pepper. Freezes well for up to 3 months.

content.title: Salmon & Potato Chowder
ingredients: 1½ lbs salmon fillet, skinless and cubed
instructions: Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Cook onion and garlic for 5 minutes.
Add potatoes and broth. Simmer for 12 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Add corn and dill. Cook for 3 minutes.
Remove from heat. Stir in Greek yogurt until smooth.
Add salmon to the hot soup. Cover and let sit for 4–5 minutes until salmon is just cooked through.
Season with salt and pepper. Gently break salmon into large flakes before serving.

content.title: Lentil & Spinach Soup
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Cook onion, garlic, and carrots for 5 minutes.
Add cumin, turmeric, and smoked paprika. Cook for 30 seconds.
Add lentils, diced tomatoes, and broth. Bring to a boil.
Reduce to a simmer and cook for 20–25 minutes until lentils are completely tender.
Stir in spinach and lemon juice. Cook for 2 more minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
content.notes: Note: Serve with a side of Greek yogurt or a hard-boiled egg to bring total protein to 28g+.

content.title: Chicken Tortilla Soup
ingredients: 1 can (14.5 oz) diced tomatoes with green chilies
Toppings: Greek yogurt, avocado, crushed baked tortilla chips
instructions: Add chicken, black beans, diced tomatoes, corn, broth, onion, chili powder, cumin, and garlic powder to a large pot. Bring to a boil.
Reduce to a simmer. Cook for 20 minutes until chicken is fully cooked.
Remove chicken and shred with two forks. Return to the soup.
Season with salt and pepper. Simmer for 5 more minutes.
Serve topped with Greek yogurt (instead of sour cream), avocado, and a small handful of crushed baked tortilla chips.

content.title: Black Bean & Beef Soup
ingredients: 2 cans (15 oz each) black beans, drained
instructions: Brown ground beef with onion and garlic in a large pot over medium-high heat. Drain fat.
Add cumin, chili powder, and smoked paprika. Stir for 1 minute.
Add black beans, diced tomatoes, and beef broth. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Stir in lime juice before serving. Season with salt and pepper.

content.title: Creamy Chicken & Mushroom Soup
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Cook onion, garlic, and mushrooms for 8 minutes until softened.
Add chicken cubes. Cook for 5–6 minutes until lightly browned.
Add broth, thyme, and rosemary. Simmer for 15 minutes until chicken is fully cooked.
Remove from heat. Stir in blended cottage cheese until smooth and creamy. Do not return to a boil.
Season with salt and pepper.
content.notes: The reason most diets fail is not dinner. It is dessert, or more precisely, the total absence of anything sweet that eventually breaks every plan. These 8 treats are built to give you real satisfaction without sending your progress backward. Every one uses protein-rich ingredients that turn indulgence into a strategy. Dominant protein sources: dairy and eggs.

content.title: Chocolate Peanut Butter Protein Fudge
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together peanut butter, Greek yogurt, honey, cocoa powder, vanilla, and salt until smooth.
Line an 8×8-inch baking pan with parchment paper.
Pour the mixture into the pan and smooth the top.
Freeze for at least 2 hours until firm.
Lift out using the parchment paper and cut into 16 equal squares.
Store in the freezer in a sealed container. Let sit at room temperature for 3 minutes before eating.
content.notes: Note: Pair with a cup of Greek yogurt to reach 25g+ protein.

content.title: Lemon Cheesecake Protein Bars
instructions: In a food processor, blend the crust ingredients until crumbly. Press firmly into the bottom of a parchment-lined 8×8-inch pan.
Blend cottage cheese, honey, lemon juice, lemon zest, and vanilla until completely smooth.
Pour over the crust and smooth the top.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight until firm.
Cut into 12 bars. Store in the fridge for up to 5 days.
content.notes: Note: Eat 2 bars and pair with a cup of Greek yogurt for a full 25g+ protein dessert.

content.title: Greek Yogurt Bark with Berries
instructions: Mix Greek yogurt with honey until smooth.
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Spread the yogurt in an even layer about ½ inch thick.
Scatter berries, sliced almonds, and chia seeds over the surface.
Freeze for at least 3 hours until completely solid.
Break into irregular pieces. Store in a sealed bag in the freezer for up to 2 months.
Eat straight from the freezer — it thaws within 1–2 minutes.
content.notes: Note: Two servings = 26g protein.

content.title: Cottage Cheese Chocolate Mousse
ingredients: Optional toppings: fresh raspberries, dark chocolate shavings
instructions: Combine cottage cheese, cocoa powder, honey, vanilla, and salt in a blender or food processor.
Blend until completely smooth and creamy — this takes about 60 seconds. Scrape down the sides and blend again.
Taste and adjust sweetness.
Divide into 4 small containers or glasses.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving. The mousse thickens as it chills.
Top with raspberries or chocolate shavings just before eating.
content.notes: Note: Pair with 2 protein balls or a glass of milk to hit 25g+.

content.title: Banana Protein Mug Cake
instructions: In a large microwave-safe mug, mash the banana until smooth.
Add the egg, Greek yogurt, peanut butter, rolled oats, baking powder, and cinnamon. Stir until well combined.
Microwave on high for 90 seconds. Check for doneness — the cake should pull away from the sides and not look wet in the center. Microwave for 15–30 seconds more if needed.
Let cool for 1 minute before eating.
Top with a dollop of Greek yogurt or a drizzle of honey if desired.
content.notes: Note: Add a side of Greek yogurt to bring total to 30g+ protein.

content.title: Peanut Butter Protein Cookies
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Mix all ingredients in a bowl until smooth.
Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto the baking sheet, spacing them 2 inches apart.
Flatten slightly with a fork in a crisscross pattern.
Bake for 10–12 minutes until the edges are golden but the centers still look slightly underdone — they firm up as they cool.
Cool completely before storing. Refrigerate for up to 1 week or freeze for up to 1 month.
content.notes: Note: Pair with a cup of milk or Greek yogurt for a 28g protein snack.

content.title: Chocolate Protein Energy Bites
instructions: Mix all ingredients in a bowl until fully combined. The mixture should hold together when pressed.
If too sticky, chill the mixture for 15 minutes before rolling.
Roll into 20 equal balls.
Place on a parchment-lined tray and refrigerate for 30 minutes until firm.
Transfer to an airtight container. Store in the fridge for up to 1 week or freeze for up to 1 month.
content.notes: Note: Pair with a cup of Greek yogurt for a satisfying 25g protein dessert.

content.title: Vanilla Cottage Cheese Parfait
instructions: In a bowl, stir together the cottage cheese, vanilla extract, and honey until well combined.
In a glass or jar, layer ½ of the vanilla cottage cheese on the bottom.
Add a layer of granola, then sliced strawberries.
Repeat the layers. Top with pecans.
For meal prep, store cottage cheese and granola separately. Assemble just before eating to keep the granola crunchy.
content.notes: Here is the real secret to never getting bored with meal prep: you do not need a hundred different proteins. You need the same great proteins paired with six sauces that completely transform the flavor. The same grilled chicken breast becomes a Greek bowl, an Asian stir-fry, a Caesar wrap, or an Italian skillet depending entirely on the sauce. This chapter is what separates the people who quit after two weeks from the people who are still going at month six.

content.title: Chipotle Lime Marinade
ingredients: 2 chipotle peppers in adobo sauce, minced
instructions: Whisk all ingredients together in a small bowl until fully combined.
Use as a marinade for chicken, beef, or shrimp — coat protein and let marinate for at least 30 minutes (up to 24 hours in the fridge).
Also works as a finishing sauce: drizzle over cooked protein and rice bowls.
Store in an airtight jar in the fridge for up to 1 week.

content.title: Greek Yogurt Tzatziki
ingredients: 1 cup cucumber, grated and squeezed dry
instructions: Grate the cucumber. Wrap in a clean towel and squeeze out as much liquid as possible. This step is essential — too much water makes the tzatziki runny.
In a bowl, combine Greek yogurt, squeezed cucumber, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, and dill.
Stir well and season with salt and pepper.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving.
Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 4 days.

content.title: High-Protein Caesar Dressing
ingredients: ¼ cup Parmesan cheese, finely grated
1 tsp anchovy paste (optional but highly recommended)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Whisk all ingredients together in a bowl until smooth and creamy.
Taste and adjust lemon and salt.
If the dressing is too thick, add water one tablespoon at a time until the desired consistency is reached.
Store in a sealed jar in the fridge for up to 5 days. Stir well before each use.

content.title: Lemon Garlic Herb Sauce
ingredients: 1 tbsp fresh thyme (or 1 tsp dried thyme)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl or jar. Whisk or shake until fully mixed.
Taste and adjust lemon and salt.
Use as a marinade before cooking or as a finishing drizzle over cooked proteins and vegetables.
Store in the fridge for up to 1 week. Bring to room temperature and shake before use.

content.title: Peanut Sauce
ingredients: 1 tsp fresh ginger, grated (or ½ tsp ground)
instructions: Whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, honey, sesame oil, ginger, and garlic in a bowl.
Add warm water one tablespoon at a time until the sauce reaches a pourable consistency.
Taste and adjust — more lime for brightness, more soy for salt, more honey for sweetness.
Store in a sealed jar in the fridge for up to 1 week.

content.title: Avocado Lime Crema
instructions: Blend or mash all ingredients until smooth and creamy.
Taste and adjust lime and salt.
Press a piece of plastic wrap directly onto the surface of the crema before sealing to prevent browning.
Store in the fridge for up to 3 days.
